newsSince: No new fundamental disclosure since June 9. Items are a trade-show presence (Silicon Saxony Days, June 15-17) and reiterated 2026-ramp guidance (targeting record orders/sales/FCF, ~33.5% EBITDA margin) that was already communicated; no fresh order or earnings data. The +9.9% move tracks the broad semicap rally rather than a company-specific catalyst.

sources.label: VAT at Silicon Saxony Days 2026 (VAT Group)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent under-covered AI supply chain: VAT is the dominant vacuum-valve monopoly (>70% share) levered to advanced-node intensity. Only fresh items are a trade show and reiterated 2026-ramp guidance - a soft positive backdrop, not a new datapoint. Quality/discovery cap alpha.
verdicts.bull: Near-monopoly vacuum-valve supplier to semicap; 2026 ramp targeting record orders/sales/FCF and rising advanced-node intensity.
verdicts.neutral: Strategically vital but well-discovered and richly valued; aftermarket adds stability without surprise.
verdicts.bear: High-quality valuation and broad semicap discovery limit residual alpha; cyclical capex exposure.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Semicap capex up-cycle and advanced-node intensity driving valve content.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Premium valuation and crowding cap upside; capex cyclicality.
